docs: The Indian Penal Code of 1860 defines murder under Section 302 with punishment of death or life imprisonment.
The Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) replaced the Indian Penal Code in 2024 and defines murder under Section 101.
The Criminal Procedure Code of 1973 governed criminal trials in India for over five decades.
The Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) replaced the Criminal Procedure Code in 2024.
Under the Indian Evidence Act of 1872, digital evidence was not originally admissible in court.
The Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam of 2024 replaced the Indian Evidence Act and explicitly recognises electronic records as primary evidence.
queries: Which law currently governs murder charges in India?
Is digital evidence admissible in Indian courts?
